PASTA WITH SUMMER VEGETABLES WITH LEMON PARMESAN SAUCE;IngredientsThe Pasta & Vegetables8 oz short pasta (like penne
column6: or rotini)1 tbsp olive oil1 medium zucchini
column7: sliced into half-moons1 medium yellow squash
column8: sliced into half-moons1 cup cherry tomatoes
column9: halved1 cup fresh or frozen peas3 cloves garlic
column10: mincedThe Lemon Parmesan Sauce2 tbsp unsalted butter1 large lemon (both zested and juiced)˝ cup freshly grated Parmesan cheese (plus more for serving)˝ cup reserved pasta waterĽ cup fresh basil leaves
column11: chopped or tornSalt and black pepper to tasteOptional: A pinch of crushed red pepper flakesStep-by-Step InstructionsCook the Pasta: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook your pasta according to the package directions until it is just al dente.Save the Liquid Gold: Before draining the pasta
column12: carefully scoop out and reserve at least ˝ cup of the starchy pasta water. Drain the pasta and set it aside.Sauté the Summer Veggies: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the zucchini and yellow squash
column13: sautéing for 3 to 4 minutes until they begin to soften and slightly brown. Stir in the minced garlic
column16: cooking for another 1 to 2 minutes until the garlic is fragrant and the tomatoes just begin to soften. Season lightly with salt and pepper
column17: then transfer the veggies to a plate.Build the Emulsified Sauce: Turn the skillet heat down to low. Melt the butter in the pan
column18: then stir in the lemon juice
column20: and half of your reserved pasta water. Whisk in the grated Parmesan cheese until it melts smoothly into the liquid.Toss and Finish: Add the cooked pasta and sautéed vegetables back into the skillet. Toss everything together vigorously over low heat so the cheese
column22: and pasta starch merge into a glossy sauce that clings to the noodles. If the pasta looks too dry
column23: splash in a little more of your reserved pasta water.Garnish and Serve: Turn off the heat. Stir in the fresh basil and optional red pepper flakes. Divide among bowls and finish with an extra sprinkle of Parmesan cheese and a crack of fresh black pepper. This recipe makes 4 servings
